lifecycle.name: NIGHTLY_AUTONOMOUS_RUN
lifecycle.summary: On-demand pre-flight gate for GitHub issues labeled autonomously-nightly. Scans open issues, assesses completeness, then either activates autonomous mode on the standard workflow or posts clarifying questions via the comment queue and exits.

phase.name: DETECT

actions.action: Query GitHub for open issues labeled "autonomously-nightly": gh issue list --label "autonomously-nightly" --state open --json number,title,body,labels,createdAt --limit 20 If the command fails, report the error and exit. If no issues are returned, report that no issues are labeled for nightly processing and exit.
Select the oldest issue by createdAt (one-at-a-time processing). Store the issue number, title, and body for subsequent phases.
Check the issue state. If the issue is OPEN, proceed to ASSESS.

If the issue is CLOSED (all work done): Read the GitHub issue comments to check for follow-up work: gh issue view {number} --json comments --jq '.comments[].body' Scan the comments for: - New feature requests or enhancements beyond the original scope - Bug reports or issues with the completed work - Explicit requests for additional changes - Review feedback that requires code changes Ignore: - Bot comments, CI status updates - Comments that are purely informational or acknowledgments - Comments that predate the issue's close date

If follow-up work IS found in the comments: Create a new GitHub issue for the follow-up work: gh issue create --title "Follow-up: {original title}" --body "{follow-up description}" --label "autonomously-nightly" Proceed to ASSESS with the new issue.

If NO follow-up work is found (issue is closed, work is done): Remove the "autonomously-nightly" label so the issue is not picked up again: gh issue edit {number} --remove-label "autonomously-nightly" Report that the issue was cleaned up and exit.

phase.on-failure: Exit — no labeled issues found or command error.

phase.name: ASSESS

actions.action: Apply the autonomous-nightly behavior's ticket-assessment rules to the issue content retrieved in DETECT.
Determine whether the issue has sufficient information for autonomous execution. Produce a verdict: COMPLETE or INCOMPLETE.

phase.on-success: If COMPLETE, proceed to ROUTE_COMPLETE. If INCOMPLETE, proceed to ROUTE_INCOMPLETE.
phase.on-failure: Exit and report the assessment failure.

phase.name: ROUTE_INCOMPLETE

actions.action: Apply the autonomous-nightly behavior's insufficient-information rules: formulate questions, then write them to the comment queue file (.github/comment-queue.json) so they appear as github-actions[bot] on the next push.

phase.on-success: Exit — questions queued, no further action.
phase.on-failure: Report the failure to post questions and exit.

phase.name: ROUTE_COMPLETE

actions.action: Activate autonomous mode — all human gates in the standard workflow are auto-approved for this issue. The following configuration is pre-set and not prompted: - Scope: single issue (current issue only). - Docs phase: run normally (scout, audit if needed, auto-approve changes).
Apply the autonomous-nightly behavior's pr-merge-rules for PR handling.
Log the autonomous mode activation in the status output.
actions.note: Activating autonomous mode also activates sub-agent dispatch mode for IMPLEMENTATION (see CLAUDE.md dispatch-mode). Agent Teams will not be used — all implementation sub-tasks use blocking Task calls.

phase.on-success: Proceed to the standard ENTRY lifecycle flow. The standard sequence (ENTRY branch setup, PLANNING, IMPLEMENTATION, DOCS, PR) runs with all human gates auto-approved.
phase.on-failure: Report the failure and exit. Do not proceed to the standard workflow.
